---
title: "Where do exact trigonometric values come from?"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: "Contents"
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    html-math-method: mathjax
  pdf:
    toc: true
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
    fontsize: 11pt
    colorlinks: true
    fig-align: center
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{tikz}
        \usetikzlibrary{angles, quotes, calc}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage{array}
        \definecolor{valblue}{RGB}{24,95,165}
        \definecolor{valteal}{RGB}{15,110,86}
        \definecolor{valamber}{RGB}{133,79,11}
        \definecolor{valpurple}{RGB}{83,74,183}
        \definecolor{valcoral}{RGB}{153,60,29}
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

There are really only **two triangles** you need. Together they unlock all the "nice" exact values — no memorisation required, just geometry you can draw yourself.

---

## Triangle 1 — the 30-60-90 triangle

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## The idea
Start with an equilateral triangle (all sides = 2, all angles = 60°). Slice it perfectly down the middle. You get a right triangle whose three sides you already know exactly.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{=html}
<div style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 260" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arr" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="context-stroke" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <rect x="40" y="20" width="200" height="220" rx="12" fill="#EEEDFE" stroke="#534AB7" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="140" y="44" text-anchor="middle" fill="#3C3489">Step 1: equilateral triangle</text>
  <polygon points="140,58 62,215 218,215" fill="none" stroke="#534AB7" stroke-width="1.8"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="72" y="188" text-anchor="end" fill="#534AB7">2</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="208" y="188" fill="#534AB7">2</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="140" y="234" text-anchor="middle" fill="#534AB7">2</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="68" y="148" text-anchor="end" fill="#534AB7">60°</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="132" y="76" text-anchor="middle" fill="#534AB7">60°</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="212" y="148" fill="#534AB7">60°</text>

  <line x1="268" y1="135" x2="316" y2="135" stroke="#888780" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="292" y="124" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">cut!</text>

  <rect x="336" y="20" width="300" height="220" rx="12" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="486" y="44" text-anchor="middle" fill="#085041">Step 2: right triangle (30-60-90)</text>

  <polygon points="396,60 396,215 556,215" fill="none" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="1.8"/>
  <rect x="396" y="198" width="17" height="17" fill="none" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="1"/>

  <path d="M396,195 A22,22 0 0,1 416,215" fill="none" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.3"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="424" y="210" fill="#185FA5">30°</text>

  <path d="M396,80 A20,20 0 0,1 414,63" fill="none" stroke="#854F0B" stroke-width="1.3"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="416" y="80" fill="#854F0B">60°</text>

  <line x1="396" y1="60" x2="396" y2="215" stroke="#0F6E56" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/>

  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="382" y="142" text-anchor="end" fill="#0F6E56">√3</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="476" y="234" text-anchor="middle" fill="#993C1D">1</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="492" y="130" fill="#534AB7">2</text>

  <line x1="560" y1="215" x2="600" y2="215" stroke="#aaa" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="604" y="219" fill="#993C1D">half the base</text>
  <line x1="560" y1="135" x2="600" y2="135" stroke="#aaa" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="604" y="139" fill="#534AB7">original side</text>
  <line x1="390" y1="46" x2="600" y2="80" stroke="#aaa" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="604" y="84" fill="#0F6E56">by Pythagoras</text>
</svg>
</div>
```
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.25]
  \coordinate (A) at (0,0);
  \coordinate (B) at (4,0);
  \coordinate (C) at (2,3.464);
  \coordinate (M) at (2,0);
  \draw[thick, color=valpurple] (A) -- (B) -- (C) -- cycle;
  \node[below left, color=valpurple, font=\small] at ($(A)!0.5!(C)$) {$2$};
  \node[below right, color=valpurple, font=\small] at ($(B)!0.5!(C)$) {$2$};
  \node[below, color=valpurple, font=\small] at ($(A)!0.5!(B)$) {$2$};
  \node[above, color=valpurple, font=\small] at (C) {$60°$};
  \node[below left, color=valpurple, font=\small] at (A) {$60°$};
  \node[below right, color=valpurple, font=\small] at (B) {$60°$};
  \draw[dashed, thick, color=valteal] (C) -- (M) node[midway, right, color=valteal, font=\small] {cut!};
  \draw (M) rectangle ++(0.15,0.15);
  \node[below, color=valcoral, font=\small] at ($(A)!0.5!(M)$) {$1$};
  \node[left, color=valteal, font=\small] at ($(A)!0.5!(C)+(0.1,0)$) {};
\end{tikzpicture}
\hspace{1.5cm}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.25]
  \coordinate (A) at (0,0);
  \coordinate (B) at (2,0);
  \coordinate (C) at (0,3.464);
  \draw[thick, color=valteal] (A) -- (B) -- (C) -- cycle;
  \draw (A) rectangle ++(0.18,0.18);
  \pic[draw=valblue, angle radius=0.7cm, "$30°$"', angle eccentricity=1.7, font=\small] {angle=C--B--A};
  \pic[draw=valamber, angle radius=0.6cm, "$60°$", angle eccentricity=1.7, font=\small] {angle=B--C--A};
  \node[left, color=valteal, font=\small\bfseries] at ($(A)!0.5!(C)$) {$\sqrt{3}$};
  \node[below, color=valcoral, font=\small\bfseries] at ($(A)!0.5!(B)$) {$1$};
  \node[right, color=valpurple, font=\small\bfseries] at ($(B)!0.5!(C)$) {$2$};
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```
:::

### What we can read off

Because the **30°** angle sees opposite = 1 and hypotenuse = 2:

$$\sin(30°) = \frac{1}{2} \qquad \cos(30°) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \qquad \tan(30°) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$$

Because the **60°** angle sees opposite = √3 and hypotenuse = 2:

$$\sin(60°) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \qquad \cos(60°) = \frac{1}{2} \qquad \tan(60°) = \sqrt{3}$$

::: {.callout-tip}
## Notice
Sin and cos simply **swap** between 30° and 60°. What is "opposite" for one angle is "adjacent" for the other — they share the same triangle!
:::

---

## Triangle 2 — the 45-45-90 triangle

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## The idea
Take a square with side = 1 and cut it along the diagonal. Both legs stay equal (= 1), and the hypotenuse follows from Pythagoras: √(1² + 1²) = √2.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{=html}
<div style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 240" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arr2" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="context-stroke" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <rect x="40" y="20" width="210" height="200" rx="12" fill="#FAEEDA" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="145" y="44" text-anchor="middle" fill="#633806">Step 1: unit square</text>
  <rect x="65" y="58" width="150" height="150" fill="none" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="1.6"/>
  <line x1="65" y1="58" x2="215" y2="208" stroke="#BA7517" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="140" y="222" text-anchor="middle" fill="#854F0B">side = 1</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="52" y="135" text-anchor="end" fill="#854F0B">1</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="140" y="56" text-anchor="middle" fill="#854F0B">1</text>

  <line x1="275" y1="130" x2="323" y2="130" stroke="#888780" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3" marker-end="url(#arr2)"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="299" y="119" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">fold!</text>

  <rect x="343" y="20" width="300" height="200" rx="12" fill="#FAECE7" stroke="#993C1D" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="493" y="44" text-anchor="middle" fill="#712B13">Step 2: right triangle (45-45-90)</text>

  <polygon points="383,200 533,200 533,58" fill="none" stroke="#993C1D" stroke-width="1.8"/>
  <rect x="516" y="183" width="17" height="17" fill="none" stroke="#993C1D" stroke-width="1"/>

  <path d="M400,200 A20,20 0 0,1 383,183" fill="none" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.3"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="408" y="192" fill="#185FA5">45°</text>

  <path d="M533,76 A20,20 0 0,0 516,59" fill="none" stroke="#185FA5" stroke-width="1.3"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="498" y="78" fill="#185FA5">45°</text>

  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="456" y="220" text-anchor="middle" fill="#993C1D">1</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="545" y="132" fill="#993C1D">1</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" x="448" y="118" text-anchor="middle" fill="#534AB7">√2</text>

  <line x1="560" y1="200" x2="600" y2="200" stroke="#aaa" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="604" y="204" fill="#993C1D">= 1 (square side)</text>
  <line x1="560" y1="58" x2="600" y2="100" stroke="#aaa" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="604" y="104" fill="#534AB7">= √(1²+1²)</text>
</svg>
</div>
```
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.3]
  \draw[thick, color=valamber] (0,0) rectangle (3,3);
  \draw[dashed, thick, color=valcoral] (0,0) -- (3,3) node[midway, above left, font=\small, color=valcoral] {diagonal};
  \node[below, font=\small, color=valamber] at (1.5,0) {$1$};
  \node[left, font=\small, color=valamber] at (0,1.5) {$1$};
\end{tikzpicture}
\hspace{1.5cm}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.3]
  \coordinate (A) at (0,0);
  \coordinate (B) at (3,0);
  \coordinate (C) at (3,3);
  \draw[thick, color=valcoral] (A) -- (B) -- (C) -- cycle;
  \draw (B) rectangle ++(-0.2,0.2);
  \pic[draw=valblue, angle radius=0.7cm, "$45°$"', angle eccentricity=1.7, font=\small] {angle=C--A--B};
  \pic[draw=valblue, angle radius=0.7cm, "$45°$"', angle eccentricity=1.7, font=\small] {angle=A--C--B};
  \node[below, color=valcoral, font=\small\bfseries] at ($(A)!0.5!(B)$) {$1$};
  \node[right, color=valcoral, font=\small\bfseries] at ($(B)!0.5!(C)$) {$1$};
  \node[left, color=valpurple, font=\small\bfseries] at ($(A)!0.5!(C)$) {$\sqrt{2}$};
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```
:::

### What we can read off

Because both legs are equal (= 1) and the hypotenuse = √2:

$$\sin(45°) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \qquad \cos(45°) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \qquad \tan(45°) = 1$$

::: {.callout-tip}
## Notice
Sin and cos are **equal** at 45° — forced by symmetry. The triangle is an isosceles right triangle, so opposite and adjacent are always the same length.
:::

---

## The boundary angles — no triangle needed

At 0° the triangle collapses flat; at 90° it stands perfectly upright.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:1rem; margin:1.5rem 0;">
  <div style="border:0.5px solid var(--color-border-tertiary); border-radius:12px; padding:1rem; background:var(--color-background-secondary); text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:13px; font-weight:500; margin-bottom:0.8rem; color:var(--color-text-secondary)">0° — triangle collapses flat</div>
    <svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 200 60">
      <line x1="20" y1="40" x2="180" y2="40" stroke="#888780" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <circle cx="20" cy="40" r="4" fill="#888780"/>
      <circle cx="180" cy="40" r="4" fill="#888780"/>
      <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="100" y="58" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">opposite = 0, adjacent = hyp</text>
    </svg>
    <div style="font-size:13px; margin-top:0.5rem; color:var(--color-text-primary)">sin 0° = 0 &nbsp;·&nbsp; cos 0° = 1</div>
  </div>
  <div style="border:0.5px solid var(--color-border-tertiary); border-radius:12px; padding:1rem; background:var(--color-background-secondary); text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:13px; font-weight:500; margin-bottom:0.8rem; color:var(--color-text-secondary)">90° — triangle stands upright</div>
    <svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 200 60">
      <line x1="100" y1="8" x2="100" y2="52" stroke="#888780" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <circle cx="100" cy="8" r="4" fill="#888780"/>
      <circle cx="100" cy="52" r="4" fill="#888780"/>
      <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="100" y="65" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">opposite = hyp, adjacent = 0</text>
    </svg>
    <div style="font-size:13px; margin-top:0.5rem; color:var(--color-text-primary)">sin 90° = 1 &nbsp;·&nbsp; cos 90° = 0</div>
  </div>
</div>
```
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
| Angle | Geometry | sin | cos | tan |
|-------|----------|-----|-----|-----|
| 0° | Triangle collapses flat | 0 | 1 | 0 |
| 90° | Triangle stands upright | 1 | 0 | undefined |
:::

---

## The secret counting pattern

Look at the sin column — it follows a beautiful hidden sequence. Write everything as $\dfrac{\sqrt{n}}{2}$ for $n = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4$:

$$\sin(0°) = \frac{\sqrt{0}}{2} \quad \sin(30°) = \frac{\sqrt{1}}{2} \quad \sin(45°) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \quad \sin(60°) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \quad \sin(90°) = \frac{\sqrt{4}}{2}$$

The cosine column is the **same list read backwards**.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{=html}
<div style="margin:1.5rem 0;">
<svg width="100%" viewBox="0 0 680 130" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arr3" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="context-stroke" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="40" y="30" fill="#5F5E5A">sin →</text>
  <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" x="40" y="100" fill="#5F5E5A">cos →</text>

  <g>
    <rect x="88" y="12" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#85B7EB" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="133" y="30" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">0°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="133" y="46" text-anchor="middle" fill="#185FA5">√0 / 2 = 0</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="180" y1="34" x2="196" y2="34" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="198" y="12" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#85B7EB" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="243" y="30" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">30°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="243" y="46" text-anchor="middle" fill="#185FA5">√1 / 2 = ½</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="290" y1="34" x2="306" y2="34" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="308" y="12" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#85B7EB" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="353" y="30" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">45°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="353" y="46" text-anchor="middle" fill="#185FA5">√2 / 2</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="400" y1="34" x2="416" y2="34" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="418" y="12" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#85B7EB" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="463" y="30" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">60°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="463" y="46" text-anchor="middle" fill="#185FA5">√3 / 2</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="510" y1="34" x2="526" y2="34" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="528" y="12" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E6F1FB" stroke="#85B7EB" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="573" y="30" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">90°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="573" y="46" text-anchor="middle" fill="#185FA5">√4 / 2 = 1</text>
  </g>

  <g>
    <rect x="88" y="78" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#5DCAA5" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="133" y="96" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">0°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="133" y="112" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0F6E56">√4 / 2 = 1</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="180" y1="100" x2="196" y2="100" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="198" y="78" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#5DCAA5" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="243" y="96" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">30°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="243" y="112" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0F6E56">√3 / 2</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="290" y1="100" x2="306" y2="100" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="308" y="78" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#5DCAA5" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="353" y="96" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">45°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="353" y="112" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0F6E56">√2 / 2</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="400" y1="100" x2="416" y2="100" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="418" y="78" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#5DCAA5" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="463" y="96" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">60°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="463" y="112" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0F6E56">√1 / 2 = ½</text>
  </g>
  <line x1="510" y1="100" x2="526" y2="100" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="1" marker-end="url(#arr3)"/>
  <g>
    <rect x="528" y="78" width="90" height="44" rx="8" fill="#E1F5EE" stroke="#5DCAA5" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" x="573" y="96" text-anchor="middle" fill="#5F5E5A">90°</text>
    <text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" x="573" y="112" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0F6E56">√0 / 2 = 0</text>
  </g>
</svg>
</div>
```
:::

---

## Complete reference table

| Angle | sin | cos | tan | Source |
|------:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:-------|
| 0° | $0$ | $1$ | $0$ | collapsing triangle |
| 30° | $\dfrac{1}{2}$ | $\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ | $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$ | equilateral triangle ÷ 2 |
| 45° | $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ | $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ | $1$ | folded square |
| 60° | $\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ | $\dfrac{1}{2}$ | $\sqrt{3}$ | equilateral triangle ÷ 2 |
| 90° | $1$ | $0$ | undefined | upright triangle |

: Exact trigonometric values and their geometric origins {.striped tbl-colwidths="[12,15,15,15,43]"}

::: {.callout-important}
## The one thing to remember
You only need to remember **two shapes**: an equilateral triangle cut in half, and a square folded diagonally. Every exact value in the table follows from those two constructions — you can always re-derive the whole table from scratch if you forget a value.
:::
